title: Candidate pairwise query-conditioned token stats MLP projection-fix rerun

queries: learned token statistics scorer candidate ranking non autoregressive multiclass
query conditioned token interaction MLP scorer scalable candidate ranking

evidence.why_now: EXP153/154/155 was the first learned token-stats MLP bundle, but EXP155 failed before the first evaluation because the MLP interface was not included in the projection-initialization guard. EXP153 was clearly better on user-churn and EXP154 was neutral under the wide user-ltv replay-aware effective delta, so the family still needs one clean salt-side rerun before scientific judgment.

hypothesis: If the learned token-statistics scorer is the correct scalable follow-up to the fixed residual in EXP150, then fixing the missing query/key projection initialization should let the bundle produce a clean item-incoterms result while preserving the same finetune-only, non-autoregressive, Stage 4-compatible candidate-aware scorer.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not introduce pretrain, loss-family changes, sampling changes, or packaging changes.
No scientific change relative to EXP153/154/155; fix only the missing query/key projection initialization for the token-stats MLP scorer.
Use serial_batch_equivalent scheduling with explicit safe GPU 5,6 placement for all three representative tasks.

EXP155 is implementation-blocked rather than scientific evidence because item-incoterms failed before the first evaluation with ValueError: Query-conditioned token stats require query/key projections.
Launched on 2026-05-09 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
Fresh current-thread heartbeat automation created: monitor-stage-3-exp156-bundle.
Completed cleanly on 2026-05-09 with global verdict: failed.
Result under replay-aware effective deltas: user-churn better, user-ltv neutral, item-incoterms worse.

Because this interface only changes autocomplete multiclass behavior, the Amazon-side movement should be treated mainly as no-op rerun drift/control evidence rather than as causal support for the scorer.
The decisive item-incoterms result stayed in the same failed plateau as EXP138, EXP141, and EXP150: mrr=0.6785764663938492 versus strict baseline 0.8147880873466811.
Conclusion: learned weighting of the current token-stat feature set is not enough; do not launch another near-duplicate fixed-pooled-label or token-stat weighting variant.
